## The Code: Embedding in Python

Chapter 3 already showed how text becomes token IDs.
Here we start from those IDs and look up vectors.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="make_matrix"}
```

This helper creates a rows-by-cols grid of numbers.
The embedding matrix uses the same matrix abstraction introduced earlier, so chapter 4 does not need a new data structure.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="random_matrix"}
```

`random_matrix` fills that grid with small random values.
With the default Xavier scale, the random values are sized by $\sqrt{2/(fan_{in}+fan_{out})}$ so activations are less likely to explode or vanish.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="embed_token"}
```

The embedding of token $i$ is row $i$ of the matrix.
This is a table lookup, not a calculation over the token ID itself.

```{.python include="src/python/common.py" snippet="embed_sequence"}
```

A sequence of $T$ token IDs becomes $T$ row lookups stacked into a $[T \times d]$ matrix.
The vector helpers used to compare rows are the same ones from @sec-ch02-notation.

*Demo.*
Run with `python3 src/python/chapter_demos.py`:

```{.python include="src/python/chapter_demos.py" snippet="chapter_04"}
```

The demo starts with token IDs, creates an embedding table, and returns the embedded sequence shape.
It also compares two embedding rows with dot product and cosine similarity, reusing the chapter 2 helpers instead of repeating them here.

Figure @fig-ch04-code-embedding-lookup shows how each token ID indexes into one row of the embedding weight matrix.

![Embedding matrix lookup](images/ch02_embedding_lookup.png){#fig-ch04-code-embedding-lookup fig-alt="Embedding matrix lookup — each token ID indexes into a row of the weight matrix."}

Figure @fig-ch04-code-semantic-space shows how semantic relationships can appear as vector directions in embedding space.

![Semantic arithmetic in embedding space](images/ch02_semantic_space.png){#fig-ch04-code-semantic-space fig-alt="Semantic arithmetic in embedding space — king − man + woman ≈ queen."}
